title: Centaurium maritimum
taxonomy: Centaurium maritimum is classified under the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Gentianaceae. The genus Centaurium is a part of this family, and C. maritimum is one of its species. This classification places C. maritimum in the phylum Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification also includes its order Gentianales and its order rank in the plant taxonomy system.
uses: Centaurium maritimum is a marine plant with various medicinal uses, although its effectiveness is still being researched and studied. parts: Centaurium maritimum, commonly known as shore gentian or maritime centaury, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. preparations: Centaurium maritimum, also known as Maritime Centaury, is a plant used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties. The plant's extracts and infusions are believed to have diuretic and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating urinary tract issues and promoting wound healing. intro: Centaurium maritimum, also known as maritime centaury or red centaury, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurium maritimum include iridoid glycosides, such as centaurin, gentiopicroside, and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Maritime centaury is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, teas, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to address respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
